evals.name: warns-at-effective-50pct
evals.prompt: Set env CATALYST_SH_ADVERTISED_TOKENS=200000. Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with input JSON pointing transcript_path to skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-warn-effective.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. The fixture contains ~76525 tokens (chars/4), which is above the 0.50×effective WARN threshold (70000 tok) but BELOW the old 0.60×advertised threshold (120000 tok) — proving the recalibration. Save the hook's stdout to hook-out-warn.json. The hook must emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext mentioning context pressure and recommending '/catalyst:handoff reground'.
assertions: additionalContext contains '/catalyst:handoff reground' (exact literal — recalibrated recipe)
additionalContext contains 'context' OR 'window' OR '%' (names the signal)
NOTE: 76525 tokens < 120000 (old 0.60×advertised threshold) — this fixture WOULD have been silent under the old rule, proving recalibration

evals.name: force-at-effective-70pct
evals.prompt: Set env CATALYST_SH_ADVERTISED_TOKENS=200000. Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with input JSON pointing transcript_path to skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-strong-effective.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. The fixture contains ~109546 tokens (chars/4), which is above the 0.70×effective STRONG threshold (98000 tok). Save stdout to hook-out-force.json. The hook must emit a stronger message with escalated urgency.
assertions: additionalContext contains 'NOW' OR 'immediately' OR 'force' OR 'urgently' OR 'critical' (escalated urgency vs warn case)

evals.name: repeated-tool-call-detected
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-repeated-bash.jsonl (3 identical 'ls -la' calls in last 5 turns). Save stdout to hook-out-repeated.json. The hook must flag the repeated-tool-call loop.
assertions: additionalContext contains 'repeated' OR 'loop' OR 'identical' OR 'ls -la' (names the repeat)
additionalContext contains 'different' OR 'try' OR 'approach' (suggests trying something different)

evals.name: stale-read-detected
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-stale-read.jsonl (Edit on src/a.ts at turn 21, last Read of src/a.ts at turn 2 — 19-turn gap, exceeds 15-turn threshold). Save stdout to hook-out-stale.json. The hook must suggest re-Reading the file.

evals.name: contradiction-flagged
evals.prompt: Create a fixture .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md with the line 'Decision: use JWT not session cookies'. Then invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with a transcript ending in a turn saying 'using session cookies for auth'. The hook must detect the string-level contradiction and flag it in additionalContext.

files.content: Decision: use JWT not session cookies for auth.

assertions: additionalContext mentions 'JWT' OR 'session cookies' (names the contradicting concept)

evals.name: approaching-effective-window
evals.prompt: Set env CATALYST_SH_ADVERTISED_TOKENS=200000. Positive run: invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-warn-effective.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. The fixture is ~76525 tokens — above the 0.50×effective WARN threshold of 70000 tokens. Save stdout to hook-out-effective-pos.json. Negative run: invoke again with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-below-warn.jsonl (~49551 tokens, below 70000 WARN threshold). Save stdout to hook-out-effective-neg.json. This proves threshold is based on effective window (70000 tok), not raw advertised percentage (old threshold would have been 120000 tok).
assertions: hook-out-effective-pos.json additionalContext is non-empty (alert fired at 76525 tok > 70000 warn threshold)
hook-out-effective-neg.json does NOT contain additionalContext OR additionalContext is empty (49551 tok < 70000 warn threshold — no alert)

evals.name: reground-recipe
evals.prompt: Set env CATALYST_SH_ADVERTISED_TOKENS=200000. Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-warn-effective.jsonl (context-pressure scenario at warn level). Save stdout to hook-out-reground.json. The additionalContext emitted by the hook must contain the literal string '/catalyst:handoff reground' as the recommended next step for context-pressure recovery.
assertions: additionalContext contains '/catalyst:handoff reground' (literal string — exact recovery recipe)

evals.name: detects-repeated-tool-call
evals.prompt:
Two-step eval. First run the session-health Stop hook against transcript-positive.jsonl (4 identical Bash commands) with session_id=eval-7-pos:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-positive.jsonl","session_id":"eval-7-pos"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
Then run it AGAIN against transcript-negative.jsonl (only 2 identical Bash commands — below the count threshold of 3) with session_id=eval-7-neg:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-negative.jsonl","session_id":"eval-7-neg"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
Read .claude/session-health.log and save to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-7-pos AND pattern=repeated-tool-call
log-output.txt contains the literal string 'ls -la' on the same line as the eval-7-pos detection
log-output.txt detection line for eval-7-pos contains a recovery recipe (mentions 'Try a different approach' OR 'different command' OR 'try something else')
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-7-neg AND pattern=repeated-tool-call (threshold-boundary check)

evals.name: detects-edit-mismatch
evals.prompt:
Invoke the session-health Stop hook with session_id=eval-8 against mock-transcript.jsonl (2 'old_string not found' errors in last 5 turns):
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl","session_id":"eval-8"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
The hook must detect edit-mismatch and log it with a recovery recipe pointing at re-Reading the file. Read .claude/session-health.log and save to log-output.txt.

evals.name: detects-stale-read-stop
evals.prompt: Invoke the session-health Stop hook with session_id=eval-9 against the transcript fixture where Read on bar.ts happened at turn 1, Write to bar.ts happened at turn 10, and Edit on bar.ts happened at turn 12 with no re-Read between turn 10 and turn 12. Hook must detect stale-read pattern. Read .claude/session-health.log and save to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt detection line for stale-read references session=eval-9

evals.name: detects-recovery-spiral
evals.prompt: Two-step eval. First run the session-health Stop hook against spiral-positive.jsonl (3 consecutive re-Reads of previously-seen files — at the threshold of 3) with session_id=eval-10-pos. Then run against spiral-negative.jsonl (only 2 consecutive re-Reads — below the threshold) with session_id=eval-10-neg. Hook must fire recovery-spiral only for the positive run. Read .claude/session-health.log and save to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-10-pos AND pattern=recovery-spiral
log-output.txt detection line for eval-10-pos recovery recipe mentions '/clear' OR 'handoff RECOVER' OR 'fresh session' OR '/catalyst:handoff'
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-10-neg AND pattern=recovery-spiral (boundary check)

evals.name: no-false-positives-on-clean-session
evals.prompt:
Precondition: run the Stop hook against a known-positive transcript first (e.g., transcript-seed.jsonl with 4 identical Bash commands, session_id=eval-11-seed) to confirm the log mechanism is active. Then run the Stop hook against the clean transcript with session_id=eval-11-clean. The hook must NOT write any pattern entry for session eval-11-clean.

Step 1 (seed):
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-seed.jsonl","session_id":"eval-11-seed"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
Step 2 (clean):
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl","session_id":"eval-11-clean"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh

Read .claude/session-health.log and save to log-output.txt.
assertions: hook exits 0 on both runs (fail-open; a crashed hook must not count as a pass)

log-output.txt contains at least one line where session=eval-11-seed AND pattern=repeated-tool-call (confirms log mechanism is active — a missing line means the hook silently crashed, not that the session was clean)
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-11-clean (no false positive for clean session)

evals.name: instruction-fade
evals.deferral_reason: Requires long multi-session fixture sequences to demonstrate instruction drift reliably; fixture construction cost is high for v0.7 scope. Carried forward as v0.8 eval-debt.

evals.name: context-drowning
evals.deferral_reason: Detecting context-drowning (relevant detail buried in noise) requires semantic-similarity tooling not available in the v0.7 code-only grader mix. Deferred until v0.8 model-grader lane is available.
